meta.deck_title: HAMMER: Hamiltonian Curiosity Augmented LLM Reinforcement

primary_points: RL with verifiable rewards (RLVR) is key for enhancing LLM reasoning
Models like OpenAI-o1 and DeepSeek-R1 use RLVR
Traditional 'easy-to-hard' curriculum learning often fails
Models get stuck in local optima, can't solve harder problems

slides.title: The Problem: Local Optimization in Curriculum Learning

images.caption: Traditional 'easy-to-hard' curriculum leads to local optimization

primary_points: Naive difficulty-based training fails to improve on harder problems
Models converge to suboptimal policies that can't generalize
Need for active curriculum principles beyond simple difficulty ordering
Reinforcement Learning with Verifiable Rewards (RLVR) enhances LLM reasoning
Used by models like OpenAI-o1 and DeepSeek-R1
Problem: Traditional 'easy-to-hard' curriculum learning fails
Key Insight: Need better curriculum strategies for effective exploration

slides.title: HAMMER: Hamiltonian Curiosity Augmented LLM Reinforcement

primary_points: HAMMER: Two-component training schema for LLMs
Component 1: Semantic embedding via LLM forward propagation
Component 2: Hamiltonian curiosity order for training sequence
Goal: Transfer diversity metrics into dynamic reinforcement learning
Sentence embeddings derived from LLM's forward propagation
Captures the model's intrinsic semantic understanding
Each training sample xᵢ produces embedding eᵢ
Pairwise similarities form complete graph G = (V, E)
Complete graph G with n vertices (samples)
Hamiltonian path visits each vertex exactly once
Find path with minimum cumulative similarity
Result: Curiosity-driven training sequence
Core Idea: Transfer diversity metrics into dynamic RL
Step 1: Generate semantic embeddings via LLM forward propagation
Step 2: Compute pairwise similarity matrix
Step 3: Find Hamiltonian path with minimum cumulative similarity
secondary_points: Result: Curiosity-driven training order retains exploration
Key Benefit: Prevents premature convergence, promotes better generalization

slides.title: Key Results: HAMMER's Consistent Performance Gains

primary_points: HAMMER achieves 3–4% average accuracy improvement across benchmarks
Tested on 4 model families and two training methods (DAPO, GRPO)
Benchmarks: AIME 2024/2025, AMC 2023, OlympiadBench
secondary_points: Diversity-driven ordering consistently outperforms random shuffle baseline
Demonstrates stable convergence and stimulates model curiosity

slides.title: Main Results Table: HAMMER vs. Baseline

tables.caption: Main Results Table: HAMMER vs. Baseline

primary_points: Gains are consistent across model sizes and training methods
Largest improvements on challenging datasets (AIME 2025)
Performance remains stable as model size increases
HAMMER effectively leverages semantic similarity for training efficiency
Consistent 3–4% accuracy gains across diverse inference benchmarks
Diversity-driven ordering facilitates stable convergence and stimulates curiosity
Outperforms traditional difficulty-based curricula that suffer from local optimization
Validated across 4 model families and multiple training methods
secondary_points: Conclusion: HAMMER provides an efficient, stable data preprocessing method
